prompt: An MSP with two hundred small customers and three large enterprises designs its ServiceNow architecture. Which segmentation strategy fits the mixed customer base?
choices: Domain separation for the small customers with dedicated instances evaluated for enterprises needing full isolation
One dedicated instance per small customer with all enterprises sharing a single global domain
A single global domain for all customers with visibility controlled only by assignment groups
Separate production instances for every customer regardless of size or requirements
explanation: MSP architecture decisions weigh tenant count, isolation needs, and lifecycle independence; domain separation scales for many similar tenants while dedicated instances suit customers requiring full isolation.

prompt: A service provider structures its domain hierarchy so tenants can be grouped by service tier and region. What hierarchy design principle supports that segmentation?
choices: Model organizational groupings as intermediate parent domains with customer tenants as children beneath them
Place every tenant directly under global with no intermediate structure or grouping
Create one domain per user so segmentation follows individual accounts
Use contains relationships instead of hierarchy for all tenant placement decisions
explanation: Intermediate parent domains let providers apply shared processes and reporting by segment while each customer tenant remains isolated below its group.

prompt: Customers on premium contracts require faster response targets than standard-tier tenants sharing the same instance. How should the MSP manage SLA definitions across domains?
choices: Maintain SLA definitions as process-separated records with tenant-specific overrides per contract tier
Apply one global SLA definition to every tenant regardless of contract terms
Track response targets only in spreadsheets outside the platform to avoid domain complexity
Disable SLA engines for standard tenants so premium breaches are easier to identify
explanation: SLA definitions are process-separated, so providers can override targets per tenant domain while retaining shared defaults for common tiers.

prompt: Finance asks the MSP platform team to support per-customer billing based on service consumption. Which platform capability supports a chargeback model on a domain-separated instance?
choices: Domain and company attribution on operational records enabling per-tenant usage reporting
Removing sys_domain from task tables so billing queries run faster
A single shared report that counts all incidents without tenant segmentation
Manual monthly record counts performed by each tenant administrator
explanation: Because records carry domain and company context, providers can report consumption per tenant and feed usage data into billing and chargeback processes.

prompt: Which factors should drive an MSP's decision between domain separation and dedicated instances for a new customer? (Choose two.)
choices: The customer's isolation, compliance, and independent release cycle requirements
The provider's ability to operate shared processes and cross-customer intelligence at scale
The alphabetical position of the customer name in the account list
The number of dashboard portlets requested by the customer's executives
explanation: Architecture selection weighs customer isolation and lifecycle needs against provider efficiencies from shared operations on a domain-separated instance.
